name: lua\_atexit
description: Adds code to be executed when the script finishes or is ended by the user. Typically used to clean up after the user terminates defer() or runloop() code.
syntax: reaper.atexit(function)

name: lua\_defer
description: Adds code to be called back by REAPER. Used to create persistent ReaScripts that continue to run and respond to input, while the user does other tasks. Identical to runloop(). Note that no undo point will be automatically created when the script finishes, unless you create it explicitly.
syntax: reaper.defer(function)

name: lua\_get\_action\_context
description: is\_new\_value,filename,sectionID,cmdID,mode,resolution,val,contextstr = reaper.get\_action\_context() Returns contextual information about the script, typically MIDI/OSC input values. val will be set to a relative or absolute value depending on mode (=0: absolute mode, >0: relative modes). resolution=127 for 7-bit resolution, =16383 for 14-bit resolution. sectionID, and cmdID will be set to -1 if the script is not part of the action list. mode, resolution and val will be set to -1 if the script was not triggered via MIDI/OSC. contextstr may be empty or one of: midi:XX:YY] (one or two bytes hex) wheel|hwheel|mtvert|mthorz|mtzoom|mtrot|mediakbd]:flags key:flags:keycode osc:/msg:f=FloatValue|:s=StringValue] KBD\_OnMainActionEx (flags may include V=virtkey, S=shift, A=alt/option, C=control/command, W=win/control)
syntax: reaper.get_action_context()

name: lua\_gfx\_variables
description: The following global variables are special and will be used by the graphics system: gfx.r - current red component (0..1) used by drawing operations. gfx.g - current green component (0..1) used by drawing operations. gfx.b - current blue component (0..1) used by drawing operations. gfx.a2 - current alpha component (0..1) used by drawing operations when writing solid colors (normally ignored but useful when creating transparent images). gfx.a - alpha for drawing (1=normal). gfx.mode - blend mode for drawing. Set mode to 0 for default options. Add 1.0 for additive blend mode (if you wish to do subtractive, set gfx.a to negative and use gfx.mode as additive). Add 2.0 to disable source alpha for gfx.blit(). Add 4.0 to disable filtering for gfx.blit(). gfx.w - width of the UI framebuffer. gfx.h - height of the UI framebuffer. gfx.x - current graphics position X. Some drawing functions use as start position and update. gfx.y - current graphics position Y. Some drawing functions use as start position and update. gfx.clear - if greater than -1.0, framebuffer will be cleared to that color. the color for this one is packed RGB (0..255), i.e. red+green*256+blue*65536. The default is 0 (black). gfx.dest - destination for drawing operations, -1 is main framebuffer, set to 0..1024-1 to have drawing operations go to an offscreen buffer (or loaded image). gfx.texth - the (READ-ONLY) height of a line of text in the current font. Do not modify this variable. gfx.ext\_retina - to support hidpi/retina, callers should set to 1.0 on initialization, this value will be updated to value greater than 1.0 (such as 2.0) if retina/hidpi. On macOS gfx.w/gfx.h/etc will be doubled, but on other systems gfx.w/gfx.h will remain the same and gfx.ext\_retina is a scaling hint for drawing. gfx.mouse\_x - current X coordinate of the mouse relative to the graphics window. gfx.mouse\_y - current Y coordinate of the mouse relative to the graphics window. gfx.mouse\_wheel - wheel position, will change typically by 120 or a multiple thereof, the caller should clear the state to 0 after reading it. gfx.mouse\_hwheel - horizontal wheel positions, will change typically by 120 or a multiple thereof, the caller should clear the state to 0 after reading it. gfx.mouse\_cap - a bitfield of mouse and keyboard modifier state. Note that a script must call gfx.getchar() at least once in order to get modifier state when the mouse is not captured by the window. Bitfield bits: 1: left mouse button 2: right mouse button 4: Control (Windows) or Command (macOS) key 8: Shift key 16: Alt (Windows) or Option (macOS) key 32: Windows (Windows) or Control (macOS) key 64: middle mouse button
syntax: gfx VARIABLES

name: lua\_gfx.arc
description: Draws an arc of the circle centered at x,y, with ang1/ang2 being specified in radians.
syntax: gfx.arc(x,y,r,ang1,ang2,antialias])

name: lua\_gfx.blit
description: Copies from source (-1 = main framebuffer, or an image from gfx.loadimg() etc), using current opacity and copy mode (set with gfx.a, gfx.mode). If destx/desty are not specified, gfx.x/gfx.y will be used as the destination position. scale (1.0 is unscaled) will be used only if destw/desth are not specified. rotation is an angle in radians srcx/srcy/srcw/srch specify the source rectangle (if omitted srcw/srch default to image size) destx/desty/destw/desth specify destination rectangle (if not specified destw/desth default to srcw/srch \* scale).
syntax: gfx.blit(source, scale, rotation, srcx, srcy, srcw, srch, destx, desty, destw, desth, rotxoffs, rotyoffs])

name: lua\_gfx.blitext
description: Deprecated, use gfx.blit instead.
syntax: gfx.blitext(source,coordinatelist,rotation)

name: lua\_gfx.blurto
description: Blurs the region of the screen between gfx.x,gfx.y and x,y, and updates gfx.x,gfx.y to x,y.
syntax: gfx.blurto(x,y)

name: lua\_gfx.circle
description: Draws a circle, optionally filling/antialiasing.
syntax: gfx.circle(x,y,r,fill,antialias])

name: lua\_gfx.clienttoscreen
description: Converts the coordinates x,y to screen coordinates, returns those values.
syntax: gfx.clienttoscreen(x,y)

name: lua\_gfx.deltablit
description: Blits from srcimg(srcx,srcy,srcw,srch) to destination (destx,desty,destw,desth). Source texture coordinates are s/t, dsdx represents the change in s coordinate for each x pixel, dtdy represents the change in t coordinate for each y pixel, etc. dsdxdy represents the change in dsdx for each line. If usecliprect is specified and 0, then srcw/srch are ignored.
syntax: gfx.deltablit(srcimg,srcs,srct,srcw,srch,destx,desty,destw,desth,dsdx,dtdx,dsdy,dtdy,dsdxdy,dtdxdy,usecliprect=1])

name: lua\_gfx.dock
description: Call with v=-1 to query docked state, otherwise v>=0 to set docked state. State is &1 if docked, second byte is docker index (or last docker index if undocked). If wx-wh specified, additional values will be returned with the undocked window position/size
syntax: gfx.dock(v,wx,wy,ww,wh])

name: lua\_gfx.drawchar
description: Draws the character (can be a numeric ASCII code as well), to gfx.x, gfx.y, and moves gfx.x over by the size of the character.
syntax: gfx.drawchar(char)

name: lua\_gfx.drawnumber
description: Draws the number n with ndigits of precision to gfx.x, gfx.y, and updates gfx.x to the right side of the drawing. The text height is gfx.texth.
syntax: gfx.drawnumber(n,ndigits)

name: lua\_gfx.drawstr
description: Draws a string at gfx.x, gfx.y, and updates gfx.x/gfx.y so that subsequent draws will occur in a similar place. If flags, right ,bottom passed in: flags&1: center horizontally flags&2: right justify flags&4: center vertically flags&8: bottom justify flags&256: ignore right/bottom, otherwise text is clipped to (gfx.x, gfx.y, right, bottom)
syntax: gfx.drawstr("str",flags,right,bottom])

name: lua\_gfx.getchar
description: If char is 0 or omitted, returns a character from the keyboard queue, or 0 if no character is available, or -1 if the graphics window is not open. If char is specified and nonzero, that character's status will be checked, and the function will return greater than 0 if it is pressed. Note that calling gfx.getchar() at least once causes gfx.mouse\_cap to reflect keyboard modifiers even when the mouse is not captured. Common values are standard ASCII, such as 'a', 'A', '=' and '1', but for many keys multi-byte values are used, including 'home', 'up', 'down', 'left', 'rght', 'f1'.. 'f12', 'pgup', 'pgdn', 'ins', and 'del'. Modified and special keys can also be returned, including: Ctrl/Cmd+A..Ctrl+Z as 1..26 Ctrl/Cmd+Alt+A..Z as 257..282 Alt+A..Z as 'A'+256..'Z'+256 27 for ESC 13 for Enter ' ' for space 65536 for query of special flags, returns: &1 (supported), &2=window has focus, &4=window is visible, &8=mouse click would hit window. 65537 queries special flags but does not do the mouse click hit testing (faster). If unichar is specified, it will be set to the unicode value of the key if available (and the return value may be the unicode value or a raw key value as described above, depending). If unichar is not specified, unicode codepoints greater than 255 will be returned as 'u'<<24 + value
syntax: gfx.getchar(char, unichar])

name: lua\_gfx.getdropfile
description: Returns success,string for dropped file index idx. call gfx.dropfile(-1) to clear the list when finished.
syntax: gfx.getdropfile(idx)

name: lua\_gfx.getfont
description: Returns current font index, and the actual font face used by this font (if available).
syntax: gfx.getfont()

name: lua\_gfx.getimgdim
description: Retreives the dimensions of an image specified by handle, returns w, h pair.
syntax: gfx.getimgdim(handle)

name: lua\_gfx.getpixel
description: Returns r,g,b values 0..1] of the pixel at (gfx.x,gfx.y)
syntax: gfx.getpixel()

name: lua\_gfx.gradrect
description: Fills a gradient rectangle with the color and alpha specified. drdx-dadx reflect the adjustment (per-pixel) applied for each pixel moved to the right, drdy-dady are the adjustment applied for each pixel moved toward the bottom. Normally drdx=adjustamount/w, drdy=adjustamount/h, etc.
syntax: gfx.gradrect(x,y,w,h, r,g,b,a, drdx, dgdx, dbdx, dadx, drdy, dgdy, dbdy, dady])

name: lua\_gfx.init
description: Initializes the graphics window with title name. Suggested width and height can be specified. If window is already open, a non-empty name will re-title window, or an empty title will resize window. Once the graphics window is open, gfx.update() should be called periodically.
syntax: gfx.init("name",width,height,dockstate,xpos,ypos])

name: lua\_gfx.line
description: Draws a line from x,y to x2,y2, and if aa is not specified or 0.5 or greater, it will be antialiased.
syntax: gfx.line(x,y,x2,y2,aa])

name: lua\_gfx.lineto
description: Draws a line from gfx.x,gfx.y to x,y. If aa is 0.5 or greater, then antialiasing is used. Updates gfx.x and gfx.y to x,y.
syntax: gfx.lineto(x,y,aa])

name: lua\_gfx.loadimg
description: Load image from filename into slot 0..1024-1 specified by image. Returns the image index if success, otherwise -1 if failure. The image will be resized to the dimensions of the image file.
syntax: gfx.loadimg(image,"filename")

name: lua\_gfx.measurechar
description: Measures the drawing dimensions of a character (for example, ascii code point 97 is 'a') with the current font (as set by gfx.setfont). Returns width and height of character.
syntax: gfx.measurechar(codepoint)

name: lua\_gfx.measurestr
description: Measures the drawing dimensions of a string with the current font (as set by gfx.setfont). Returns width and height of string.
syntax: gfx.measurestr("str")

name: lua\_gfx.muladdrect
description: Multiplies each pixel by mul\_\* and adds add\_\*, and updates in-place. Useful for changing brightness/contrast, or other effects.
syntax: gfx.muladdrect(x,y,w,h,mul_r,mul_g,mul_b,mul_a,add_r,add_g,add_b,add_a])

name: lua\_gfx.printf
description: Formats and draws a string at gfx.x, gfx.y, and updates gfx.x/gfx.y accordingly (the latter only if the formatted string contains newline). For more information on format strings, see sprintf()
syntax: gfx.printf("format", ...])

name: lua\_gfx.quit
description: Closes the graphics window.
syntax: gfx.quit()

name: lua\_gfx.rect
description: Fills a rectangle at x,y, w,h pixels in dimension, filled by default.
syntax: gfx.rect(x,y,w,h,filled])

name: lua\_gfx.rectto
description: Fills a rectangle from gfx.x,gfx.y to x,y. Updates gfx.x,gfx.y to x,y.
syntax: gfx.rectto(x,y)

name: lua\_gfx.roundrect
description: Draws a rectangle with rounded corners.
syntax: gfx.roundrect(x,y,w,h,radius,antialias])

name: lua\_gfx.screentoclient
description: Converts the screen coordinates x,y to client coordinates, returns those values.
syntax: gfx.screentoclient(x,y)

name: lua\_gfx.set
description: Sets gfx.r/gfx.g/gfx.b/gfx.a/gfx.mode/gfx.a2, sets gfx.dest if final parameter specified
syntax: gfx.set(r,g,b,a,mode,dest,a2])

name: lua\_gfx.setcursor
description: Sets the mouse cursor to resource\_id and/or custom\_cursor\_name.
syntax: gfx.setcursor(resource_id,custom_cursor_name)

name: lua\_gfx.setfont
description: Can select a font and optionally configure it. idx=0 for default bitmapped font, no configuration is possible for this font. idx=1..16 for a configurable font, specify fontface such as "Arial", sz of 8-100, and optionally specify flags, which is a multibyte character, which can include 'i' for italics, 'u' for underline, or 'b' for bold. These flags may or may not be supported depending on the font and OS. After calling gfx.setfont(), gfx.texth may be updated to reflect the new average line height.
syntax: gfx.setfont(idx,"fontface", sz, flags])

name: lua\_gfx.setimgdim
description: Resize image referenced by index 0..1024-1, width and height must be 0-8192. The contents of the image will be undefined after the resize.
syntax: gfx.setimgdim(image,w,h)

name: lua\_gfx.setpixel
description: Writes a pixel of r,g,b to gfx.x,gfx.y.
syntax: gfx.setpixel(r,g,b)

name: lua\_gfx.showmenu
description: Shows a popup menu at gfx.x,gfx.y. str is a list of fields separated by | characters. Each field represents a menu item. Fields can start with special characters: # : grayed out ! : checked > : this menu item shows a submenu < : last item in the current submenu An empty field will appear as a separator in the menu. gfx.showmenu returns 0 if the user selected nothing from the menu, 1 if the first field is selected, etc. Example: gfx.showmenu("first item, followed by separator||!second item, checked|>third item which spawns a submenu|#first item in submenu, grayed out|\<second and last item in submenu|fourth item in top menu")
syntax: gfx.showmenu("str")

name: lua\_gfx.transformblit
description: Blits to destination at (destx,desty), size (destw,desth). div\_w and div\_h should be 2..64, and table should point to a table of 2*div\_w*div\_h values (table can be a regular table or (for less overhead) a reaper.array). Each pair in the table represents a S,T coordinate in the source image, and the table is treated as a left-right, top-bottom list of texture coordinates, which will then be rendered to the destination.
syntax: gfx.transformblit(srcimg,destx,desty,destw,desth,div_w,div_h,table)

name: lua\_gfx.triangle
description: Draws a filled triangle, or any convex polygon.
syntax: gfx.triangle(x1,y1,x2,y2,x3,y3x4,y4...])

name: lua\_gfx.update
description: Updates the graphics display, if opened
syntax: gfx.update()

name: lua\_gmem\_attach
description: Causes gmem\_read()/gmem\_write() to read EEL2/JSFX/Video shared memory segment named by parameter. Set to empty string to detach. 6.20+: returns previous shared memory segment name.
syntax: reaper.gmem_attach(sharedMemoryName)

name: lua\_gmem\_read
description: Read (number) value from shared memory attached-to by gmem\_attach(). index can be 0..1<<25).
syntax: reaper.gmem_read(index)

name: lua\_gmem\_write
description: Write (number) value to shared memory attached-to by gmem\_attach(). index can be 0..1<<25).
syntax: reaper.gmem_write(index,value)

name: lua\_new\_array
description: Creates a new reaper.array object of maximum and initial size size, if specified, or from the size/values of a table/array. Both size and table/array can be specified, the size parameter will override the table/array size.
syntax: reaper.new_array(table|array]size])

name: lua\_runloop
description: Adds code to be called back by REAPER. Used to create persistent ReaScripts that continue to run and respond to input, while the user does other tasks. Identical to defer(). Note that no undo point will be automatically created when the script finishes, unless you create it explicitly.
syntax: reaper.runloop(function)

name: lua\_set\_action\_options
description: reaper.set\_action\_options(flag) Sets action options for the script. flag&1: script will auto-terminate if re-launched while already running flag&2: if (flag&1) is set, script will re-launch after auto-terminating. otherwise, re-launch is ignored. flag&4: set script toggle state on flag&8: set script toggle state off
syntax: reaper.set_action_options(flag)

name: lua\_{reaper.array}.clear
description: Sets the value of zero or more items in the array. If value not specified, 0.0 is used. offset is 1-based, if size omitted then the maximum amount available will be set.
syntax: {reaper.array}.clear(value, offset, size])

name: lua\_{reaper.array}.convolve
description: Convolves complex value pairs from reaper.array, starting at 1-based srcoffs, reading/writing to 1-based destoffs. size is in normal items (so it must be even)
syntax: {reaper.array}.convolve(src, srcoffs, size, destoffs])

name: lua\_{reaper.array}.copy
description: Copies values from reaper.array or table, starting at 1-based srcoffs, writing to 1-based destoffs.
syntax: {reaper.array}.copy(src, srcoffs, size, destoffs])

name: lua\_{reaper.array}.fft
description: Performs a forward FFT of size. size must be a power of two between 4 and 32768 inclusive. If permute is specified and true, the values will be shuffled following the FFT to be in normal order.
syntax: {reaper.array}.fft(size, permute, offset])

name: lua\_{reaper.array}.fft\_realPerforms a forward FFT of size. size must be a power of two between 4 and 32768 inclusive. If permute is specified and true, the values will be shuffled following the FFT to be in normal order.
description: Performs a forward real->complex FFT of size. size must be a power of two between 4 and 32768 inclusive. If permute is specified and true, the values will be shuffled following the FFT to be in normal order.
syntax: {reaper.array}.fft_real(size, permute, offset])

name: lua\_{reaper.array}.get\_alloc
description: Returns the maximum (allocated) size of the array.
syntax: {reaper.array}.get_alloc()

name: lua\_{reaper.array}.ifft
description: Performs a backwards FFT of size. size must be a power of two between 4 and 32768 inclusive. If permute is specified and true, the values will be shuffled before the IFFT to be in fft-order.
syntax: {reaper.array}.ifft(size, permute, offset])

name: lua\_{reaper.array}.ifft\_real
description: Performs a backwards complex->real FFT of size. size must be a power of two between 4 and 32768 inclusive. If permute is specified and true, the values will be shuffled before the IFFT to be in fft-order.
syntax: {reaper.array}.ifft_real(size, permute, offset])

name: lua\_{reaper.array}.multiply
description: Multiplies values from reaper.array, starting at 1-based srcoffs, reading/writing to 1-based destoffs.
syntax: {reaper.array}.multiply(src, srcoffs, size, destoffs])

name: lua\_{reaper.array}.resize
description: Resizes an array object to size. size must be 0..max\_size].
syntax: {reaper.array}.resize(size)

name: lua\_{reaper.array}.table
description: Returns a new table with values from items in the array. Offset is 1-based and if size is omitted all available values are used.
syntax: {reaper.array}.table(offset, size])
